title: The Second Sunday after Pentecost
context:
# TEMPORAL CONTEXT SNAPSHOT
## Saint John's Episcopal Cathedral, Denver, Colorado
## June 23, 2019 | Preacher: Broderick Greer

---

LITURGICAL CONTEXT

This is the Second Sunday after Pentecost in Ordinary Time, falling within the long season extending toward Advent. The Revised Common Lectionary readings for this date would be 1 Samuel 17:1a, 4-11, 19-23, 32-49 (David and Goliath), Psalm 9:9-20, 2 Corinthians 6:1-13, and Mark 4:35-41 (Jesus calms the storm). The church was in the post-Pentecost season focused on growth and discipleship, with extended green liturgical vestments and emphasis on Christ's teachings and miracles.

---

EPISCOPAL / ANGLICAN CHURCH NEWS

The Episcopal Church was in a period of relative stability under Presiding Bishop Michael Curry, who had been elected in 2015 and was known for his emphasis on radical love and social justice. Colorado's Diocese of Colorado under Bishop Robert Wright was actively engaged in immigration advocacy and LGBTQ+ inclusion initiatives, particularly relevant in a post-2016 political landscape. No major General Convention was scheduled (the triennial would occur in July 2018 had already passed; next was 2021), so the denomination was in an interim period between legislative assemblies.

---

DENVER & COLORADO NEWS

Denver was experiencing rapid growth and gentrification pressures, with downtown housing prices continuing to climb and Capitol Hill (where Saint John's Cathedral sits) undergoing significant demographic shifts. Colorado had been in a severe drought cycle for several years, though early summer 2019 brought typical monsoon season moisture. Local focus was on the 2020 election cycle beginning to build momentum, with Colorado established as a swing state and Denver as a progressive stronghold in an increasingly purple state.

---

NATIONAL NEWS (US)

The Trump administration was in active conflict with Congress over the Southern Border crisis, with family separations still a dominant news story and the humanitarian situation at detention centers under intense scrutiny. The 2020 presidential campaign was in early motion with the second Democratic debate scheduled for later in June, featuring two dozen candidates. The Supreme Court had just ruled on gerrymandering cases and voting rights issues, and Roe v. Wade remained under pressure from multiple state abortion bans being challenged in courts.

---

INTERNATIONAL NEWS

The Hong Kong extradition bill protests were escalating dramatically in late June, with massive pro-democracy demonstrations that would define the summer of 2019. Japan was preparing to host the G20 summit (June 28-29), with trade tensions between the US and multiple countries remaining high under Trump administration tariffs. Iran was in increasing tension with the US following Trump's withdrawal from the JCPOA, with attacks on oil tankers in the Persian Gulf occurring in mid-June.

---

CULTURAL MOMENT

American culture in June 2019 was marked by streaming dominance (Game of Thrones' final season had just concluded to mixed reception), superhero fatigue after Avengers: Endgame's massive cultural moment in April, and social media intensity around identity politics and social justice debates. Denver's sports fans were in the offseason lull between NBA Finals (Toronto Raptors had just won) and the start of baseball's All-Star Game in July. The broader zeitgeist was one of political polarization, climate anxiety, and demographic anxiety about rapid urban change, particularly acute in a Colorado congregation witnessing their city's transformation.
